text.title: design-system-success-factors Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for design-system-success-factors as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent fabricating adoption percentage by counting import statements without counting render sites.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A team may import the Button once and implement 40 custom buttons locally. Import-level counting inflates coverage metrics; render-site AST scanning (ts-morph) provides the actual figure.

failure-mode.description: LLM generating components that re-implement existing system primitives because the catalogue was not provided as context.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without the system's catalogue (Storybook index.json or package exports), the agent does not know what already exists and will write duplicate components that increase snowflake usage.

failure-mode.description: Token rename PRs from agents missing CSS-in-JS template literals and Tailwind config references.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: AST token rename tools typically handle import paths and property access, but miss string-interpolated CSS-in-JS patterns and Tailwind config keys. Require a second-pass regex sweep before merging.

failure-mode.description: Agent-authored PRs merging to consumer app main branches without per-app release-train coordination.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Codemods to unrelated apps must respect their release schedules. Auto-merging into main of unrelated apps can ship breaking visual changes ahead of their release cycle.
